variant_description: v2: full UD FEATS emission and a real lemmatizer (edit-script head trained on 2.1M gold lemmas). Strict-UD categories via ALKSNIS convention fine-tune.

full_test_bench.note: For the highest slots/UPOS numbers (89.1%/92.5%) at the cost of no lemmas and slots-only FEATS, use the companion litlat-bert-lithuanian-morphology model; this model is the complete-annotation variant.
full_test_bench.protocol: 684-sentence UD_Lithuanian-ALKSNIS gold test, raw-text input, letter-token alignment

conll18_official.note: residual UFeats gap vs UDPipe is dominated by peripheral-key conventions not derivable from the training sources (Aspect, NUM Definite, Hyph)

lineage: Fully permissive: gold MATAS v3.0 (CC BY 4.0) + gold UD_Lithuanian-ALKSNIS (CC BY-SA 4.0), UD FEATS reconstructed deterministically from MATAS's Jablonskis XPOS, remaining contextual gaps filled by iterated constrained-decoding self-training (no UDPipe or other NC-licensed model anywhere in the lineage), base encoder EMBEDDIA/litlat-bert (CC BY-SA 4.0).
speed_hardware: ONNX Runtime INT8, single CPU thread pool, consumer laptop (measured alongside network-served UDPipe at 605 tok/s)
training_note: v2 trains on the full UD label space (MULTEXT-East + Jablonskis XPOS decoding fills MATAS gaps) with a joint edit-script lemma head; dev lemma accuracy 97.1%.
